title: Near Death Wish

jokes.text: Oh, don't feel bad, son. We couldn't be more proud of you. Unless, of course, you had won the award.
jokes.setup: Comforting a disappointed award nominee
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting the comfort with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expected comfort with devastating honesty, delivered in Hermes' characteristic blunt style

jokes.text: Man, you're so lucky to have your family here. The stupid Professor didn't even show up for me.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing envy about family support
jokes.punchline: Sets up the entire episode's conflict about Professor's absence
jokes.explanation: Good character setup that establishes the episode's emotional core, though not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Mike Pisarik, lion.
jokes.setup: Memorial for delivery boys who died in the line of duty
jokes.punchline: One was killed by a lion, which is absurd for a delivery job
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with absurd cause of death, economical and unexpected

jokes.text: They're on the truck to heaven now.
jokes.setup: Memorial service for dead delivery workers
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'truck' instead of traditional 'train to heaven'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that fits the delivery theme, though predictable

jokes.text: And the fish sticks were limp!
jokes.setup: Fry denouncing Professor Farnsworth's character flaws
jokes.punchline: Adding a completely irrelevant complaint about fish sticks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - escalating anger with completely random, petty complaint. Great character integration and absurdist humor

jokes.text: Ooh, hefty. You could really bash in a skull with this thing. I know, right?
jokes.setup: Examining a trophy award
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately thinking of violent uses, with casual agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - violent thoughts about innocent objects, delivered casually

jokes.text: You see, I came down with a searing case of who gives a crap.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming he had a very good reason for missing the awards
jokes.punchline: Fake medical condition name for not caring
jokes.explanation: Great Professor line - medical-sounding excuse for his cruelty, highly quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: Sure, they exhibit all the telltale signs... toenails growing, hearts pumping fluid, the whole shmagoigle.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Professor's parents are still alive
jokes.punchline: Listing basic life functions as if they're unusual signs, ending with nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating normal life signs as mysterious, with fun nonsense word

jokes.text: So where do these fossils live, in sedimentary rock?
jokes.setup: Asking where the old parents live
jokes.punchline: Pun connecting 'fossils' (old people) with geological formations
jokes.explanation: Solid geological pun that works with Bender's insulting nature

jokes.text: Oh, really? Does that rule apply to... Clippie winners?
jokes.setup: Guard saying visitors are forbidden
jokes.punchline: Fry thinking his minor delivery award grants special access
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's misplaced pride in his minor award

jokes.text: Not now, Leela, I'm trying to meet old people.
jokes.setup: Leela finding Fry's confidence attractive
jokes.punchline: Fry prioritizing meeting elderly people over romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry priorities - completely missing romantic moment for mundane goal

jokes.text: My God... It's full of geezers.
jokes.setup: Dramatic revelation moment entering retirement home
jokes.punchline: Parody of '2001: A Space Odyssey' line but with old people
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of famous sci-fi line, economical and fits the space setting

jokes.text: My God... It's full of geezers.
jokes.setup: Fry seeing the retirement facility for the first time
jokes.punchline: Parody of '2001: A Space Odyssey' line 'My God, it's full of stars'
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that works on its own and rewards those who get it

jokes.text: Is it some kind of craft-matic adjustable death bed?
jokes.setup: Seeing elderly people hooked up to machines
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Craft-matic adjustable bed' with 'death'
jokes.explanation: Good dark pun referencing real product, though somewhat expected

jokes.text: But wouldn't almost anything make a better battery than a human body? Like a potato? Or a battery?
jokes.setup: Explaining Matrix concept of humans as batteries
jokes.punchline: Pointing out obvious better alternatives, including actual batteries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of The Matrix's logic flaw, building to perfect absurdist punchline

jokes.text: It seemed like the single crummiest, laziest, most awful, dimwitted idea in the entire history of science fiction, but it turned out to be true. Who knew?
jokes.setup: Defending The Matrix concept
jokes.punchline: Admitting it was terrible but happened to be true
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on The Matrix's plot holes, with escalating insults

jokes.text: I'm gonna call them Gram-Gram and Shabba-doo.
jokes.setup: Fry seeing his ancient relatives for first time
jokes.punchline: Giving them childish, silly nicknames
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry behavior - immediately creating silly names for serious relatives

jokes.text: They look just like you, Fry. Arms, legs, ugly.
jokes.setup: Observing family resemblance
jokes.punchline: Lists normal features then adds insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender cruelty with good escalation structure

jokes.text: It's not just safe, it's 40% safe.
jokes.setup: Question about safety of virtual reality device
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature percentage gag suggesting 40% is reassuring
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender percentage joke, though well-worn by this point in series

jokes.text: Told you it was safe.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming device was 40% safe
jokes.punchline: Said while presumably smoking/sparking from electrocution
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to safety claim, though predictable

jokes.text: There's only one word for it: Terrible. And also horrible.
jokes.setup: Claiming there's only one word to describe something
jokes.punchline: Immediately using two words
jokes.explanation: Standard 'one word' joke setup, predictable but functional

jokes.text: I can smell a scam a mile away. Reverse mortgages. Get your reverse mortgages here.
jokes.setup: Grandfather claiming he can detect scams easily
jokes.punchline: Immediately falling for reverse mortgage pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect elderly character moment - boasting about scam detection while falling for stereotypical elderly scam

jokes.text: What are you, a... A junior in high school? Most recently, yes.
jokes.setup: Grandmother guessing Fry's age as high school junior
jokes.punchline: Fry's technically accurate but misleading answer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing Fry's frozen-in-time situation with clever wordplay

jokes.text: And that's how I got this scar above my eye, and this fork inside my lung.
jokes.setup: Grandfather telling stories about scars
jokes.punchline: Escalating from external scar to fork embedded in lung
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from normal injury story to absurd internal cutlery

jokes.text: Hey, look at this. I can pull my thumb off. Oh, stop, Shabba-doo, you're freaking me out.
jokes.setup: Grandfather showing off magic trick
jokes.punchline: Fry being disturbed by simple thumb trick
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy enhanced by Fry's overreaction and use of silly nickname

jokes.text: You should have seen him when he was little. My golly, he was crotchety and moldy.
jokes.setup: Describing young Professor differently than expected
jokes.punchline: He had the same unpleasant traits as a child
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke - Professor was always unpleasant

jokes.text: Prove it, loser!
jokes.setup: Discussion of rigged bingo
jokes.punchline: Elderly person using aggressive modern insult
jokes.explanation: Contrast humor between elderly setting and contemporary aggressive language

jokes.text: I don't think Gram-Gram likes that place, and I know Shabba-don't.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing concern about grandparents' living situation
jokes.punchline: Pun changing 'Shabba-doo' to 'Shabba-don't'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the silly nickname, shows Fry's affection and wordplay skills

jokes.text: I'm about to lose history's greatest life all because of some useless old people.
jokes.setup: Professor in danger, blaming his parents
jokes.punchline: Calling his own parents useless while being completely selfish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor narcissism - even when his parents are helping, he insults them

jokes.text: Turn left at Miriam Feinberg. I meant Feingold!
jokes.setup: Using elderly people as landmarks for directions
jokes.punchline: Correcting himself with another similar Jewish surname
jokes.explanation: Good elderly humor about mixing up similar names and using people as landmarks

jokes.text: I'm Dr. Zoidberg. I'm very important.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg introducing himself to the elderly relatives
jokes.punchline: Claims importance despite being universally disrespected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - desperate need for validation and status

jokes.text: Are they my zombies from hammacher schlemmer?
jokes.setup: Professor asked if special old people are here for him
jokes.punchline: Expecting zombies from upscale catalog company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke combining zombies with high-end catalog company, perfect Professor weirdness

jokes.text: Lady, all of human history happened in your past!
jokes.setup: Velma saying troubling things happened in their past
jokes.punchline: Hermes pointing out she's lived through all of history
jokes.explanation: Excellent logical observation about extreme age, delivered with Hermes' characteristic directness

jokes.text: I'd be surprised if you live it out! Whoo! You're on fire!
jokes.setup: Elderly people wanting to 'live it up'
jokes.punchline: Bender predicting their death, Fry praising the dark joke
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor from Bender with Fry's encouragement, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Gram-Gram fell down at the ice cream store and we got free ice cream!
jokes.setup: Describing their fun day out
jokes.punchline: Elderly person's injury resulting in free treats
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about profiting from elderly mishap, delivered with Fry's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: God, how I hate them!
jokes.setup: Professor wanting his parents to love him
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor contradiction - wanting love while expressing hatred, very economical

jokes.text: That's wonderful, dear. Here you go, squeakers.
jokes.setup: Young Professor showing his science project to parents
jokes.punchline: Parents dismissing it and feeding it to a pet
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing parental neglect of Professor's scientific interests

jokes.text: Not mature enough for college yet!
jokes.setup: Retelling his parents' reason for denying MIT
jokes.punchline: Professor's bitter emphasis on the age restriction
jokes.explanation: Shows Professor's lingering resentment, though not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: You're all I have left, squeakers.
jokes.setup: Professor isolated on farm with only pet for company
jokes.punchline: Pathetic declaration to what was probably his science project
jokes.explanation: Sad character moment showing young Professor's isolation and attachment to pet/project

jokes.text: Oh, I hate you, I hate you, I hate you! You ruined my life! Good-bye!
jokes.setup: Professor finally confronting his parents with his feelings
jokes.punchline: Childish repetitive tantrum despite being ancient
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's emotional immaturity despite his age

jokes.text: That is one crazy, uncircumcised old man.
jokes.setup: Reacting to Professor's nude tantrum
jokes.punchline: Random inclusion of circumcision status in assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender non-sequitur adding inappropriate detail to observation

jokes.text: He doesn't need tires.
jokes.setup: Wondering why Professor would go to Queens
jokes.punchline: Dismissing tire-buying as reason since he's naked
jokes.explanation: Logical Fry observation about nude Professor not needing car services

jokes.text: He was a crazy-ass nutjob! Thank you, ned.
jokes.setup: Velma trying to delicately explain the older brother
jokes.punchline: Ned's blunt, crude assessment and Velma's polite thanks
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Velma's diplomacy and Ned's bluntness, with polite response to crude language

jokes.text: Yeah, sweat. Right.
jokes.setup: Velma mentioning pajamas soaked with sweat from nightmares
jokes.punchline: Ned's skeptical implication it wasn't sweat
jokes.explanation: Dark humor implying wet dreams rather than night sweats, economical but somewhat crude

jokes.text: 81, thallium. 82, lead. 82, bismuth.
jokes.setup: Reading from favorite bedtime book
jokes.punchline: It's the periodic table of elements
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - science-obsessed boy's 'favorite book' is periodic table, shows family understanding

jokes.text: a mental institution! Got himself a full wackademic scholarship.
jokes.setup: Older brother being admitted to prestigious institution
jokes.punchline: It's a mental hospital with pun on 'academic scholarship'
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun combining 'wacky' and 'academic' for mental institution scholarship, very quotable

jokes.text: your online doctorate in rodeo studies. Rodeo studies?
jokes.setup: Parents explaining they got Professor an education
jokes.punchline: It's a ridiculous fake online degree in rodeo
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist degree contrasting with Professor's scientific brilliance, very quotable

jokes.text: Floyd? Who the hell's Floyd? I'm Hubert! You're Hubert? The older brother?
jokes.setup: Parents calling Professor by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Major revelation about mistaken identity throughout story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist revealing entire backstory was about mistaken identity, recontextualizes everything

jokes.text: It felt like a minute compared to grad school.
jokes.setup: Professor saying he was in mental institution for 25 years
jokes.punchline: Comparing it favorably to graduate school experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect academic humor - mental institution preferable to grad school, highly relatable and quotable

jokes.text: a homeless rodeo clown named Floyd came to the door claiming he was... Bender! Why do you always have to be the center of attention?
jokes.setup: Amy trying to resolve the Floyd mystery
jokes.punchline: Cut off by Bender making it about himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Bender's narcissism interrupting plot resolution

jokes.text: My damn skin even hurts.
jokes.setup: Listing physical ailments from being in real world
jokes.punchline: Even skin hurts, most basic body part in pain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of elderly pain complaints to absurd level

jokes.text: we get fed through our spines and the rent is reasonable.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they want to return to virtual retirement home
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of spine feeding as a housing benefit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery of horrific sci-fi concept as mundane housing perk.

jokes.text: when they inject the holidays into our brains?
jokes.setup: Suggesting when Fry should visit again
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions horrific-sounding holiday brain injection
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark sci-fi concept - holidays as medical procedure rather than experience

jokes.text: Appearances... just a simple matter of appearances.
jokes.setup: Creating beautiful virtual farm for parents
jokes.punchline: Professor's dismissive comment about the touching gesture
jokes.explanation: Professor trying to downplay his emotional gesture, characteristic defense mechanism

jokes.text: Appearances... just a simple matter of appearances.
jokes.setup: Parents amazed by virtual recreation of their farm
jokes.punchline: Professor dismissively reduces their emotional reaction to mere visuals
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - intellectualizes emotional family moment

tmdb_overview: The Professor is deeply disturbed when his parents return from virtual retirement aboard the Near-Death Star.
